---
title: "Scholarly Communication"
toc: true
listing:
  - id: journal-articles
    contents: papers.yml
    include:
      section: "Journal Articles"
    sort: "date desc"
    template: ../../html/research/listing.ejs
  - id: preprints
    contents: papers.yml
    include:
      section: "Preprints"
    sort: "date desc"
    template: ../../html/research/listing.ejs
  - id: in-progress
    contents: papers.yml
    include:
      section: "In Progress"
    sort: "date desc"
    template: ../../html/research/listing.ejs
---

The current academic publishing system — dominated by a small number of commercial publishers — is costly, restrictive, and misaligned with the interests of science and society. This research line develops theoretical models and practical tools to support a transition toward **scholar-governed, open-access publishing**.

In 2012, I co-founded [Open Scholar](https://openscholar.org.uk), a non-profit initiative dedicated to reforming academic publishing. Our work has addressed peer review reform, overlay journals, institutional repositories, and the political economy of open access.

::: {.programme-links}
[<i class="bi bi-box-arrow-up-right"></i> OSF Project](#){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm target="_blank"}
[<i class="bi bi-github"></i> GitHub](#){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm target="_blank"}
:::

## Journal Articles

:::{#journal-articles}
:::

## Preprints

:::{#preprints}
:::

## In Progress

:::{#in-progress}
:::
